author: bharath.vedartham
content:
We might not need an SSE event for listening to proposer preferences and also a beacon api to submit the execution payload bids on the gossip.
But it is ideal to have them because some of the p2p validations require access to beacon state and it is cleaner if it is done in the beacon node. 

The biggest blocker for me to implement payload revealing is this API: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/584 since we need to compute the post state root after constructing the envelope

author: terencechain
content: Did any client implement this? Builder itself could have it internally without relying on beacon api

author: bharath.vedartham
content: No i don't think any client has implemented it. Can you let me know how a builder can do this without relying on the beacon api? I can try doing it on buildoor

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
buildoor basically uses the engine api to get payloads from the EL and caches the payload. 
One way in which we can do this without the api is to manually keep track of the beacon node state and make the state changes of `process_execution_payload` which might be a lot of duplicate code from the EL.

author: terencechain
content:
you just have to run the state transition function, `process_execution_payload` like you said. It's ~100 lines of code in Go. Depends on your language, you can copy prysm's code for it. 

i do agree beacon api may be more sensible here

author: bharath.vedartham
content: We are using golang, so i assume we can just use prysm

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
in an ideal situation, it would be great if:
1. We have an SSE event for proposer preferences
2. An API to construct the envelope

author: bharath.vedartham
content: but i believe i can just have a initial version by directly connecting to the p2p n/w and doing the process_execution_payload state transition

author: bharath.vedartham
content: and then when the apis get built clean up the codebase to use them

author: terencechain
content: is this what we need: https://github.com/OffchainLabs/prysm/pull/16522

author: bharath.vedartham
content: I think that only implements the `PublishExecutionPayloadEnvelope` API which we definitely need for the builder to publish the signed execution payload envelope, but i don't think it covers the case where a builder can provider a beacon block root, payload and execution requests and the beacon node outputs a execution payload envelope

author: bharath.vedartham
content: specifically this API: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/584 would def love more eyes on it. we might need more or less params

content:

content: or maybe its on another branch?

content: no no, it's as a question to what you said would be great. there's no implementation yet because no one proposed a spec on beacon api

author: bharath.vedartham
content: apologies i misunderstood your message! nico raised this issue: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/issues/570

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I can raise a PR for the SSE event and to get the proposer preferences from the op pool, i think these should be straightforward to spec out!
```
We also need a new api for validator clients to submit SignedProposerPreferences to the beacon node to be broadcast.
```
This one i ll leave up to the clients!  as I am not involved in that part of the impl!

author: jameshe5018
content: signed proposer preferences is implemented in grpc just not beacon api right now, there's still discussions on it generally too though..

content: ahh a lot has happened there! thanks for pointing it out!
